prompt: Say: This museum is very interesting.
hints: A simple opinion sentence.

prompt: a museum ticket
hints: Match the phrase.

prompt: Say: This statue is famous.
hints: A simple description.

prompt: the Kremlin
hints: Russia's most famous fortress and seat of government.
culturalNote: The Moscow Kremlin is a symbol of Russia and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

prompt: Say: Red Square is beautiful.
hints: 'Красная' here means beautiful/red — the word originally meant beautiful in Old Russian.
culturalNote: In Old Russian the word 'красная' meant 'beautiful', not 'red in color' — hence the name of Red Square.

prompt: a church
hints: A religious building, common across Russian towns.

prompt: Say: I like Russian art.
hints: 'Нравиться' + dative: 'Мне нравится' = I like.

prompt: Ask: Is there an entrance fee?
hints: A practical question at any museum or monument.

prompt: Say: This palace is very old.
hints: 'Дворец' is masculine — adjectives must agree: 'старый'.

prompt: a monument
hints: A structure built to honor someone or something.

prompt: ballet
hints: Russia is world-famous for this art form.
culturalNote: The Bolshoi and Mariinsky theaters are the two main centers of Russian ballet.

prompt: Say: The Bolshoi Theatre is very famous.
hints: 'Знаменит' is a short-form adjective — common in formal descriptions of places.

prompt: a nesting doll
hints: One of Russia's most iconic souvenirs — dolls inside dolls inside dolls.
culturalNote: The matryoshka doll appeared in the late 19th century and became a symbol of Russia around the world.

prompt: a samovar
hints: A traditional heated urn for brewing tea — a centrepiece of Russian hospitality.
culturalNote: The samovar has been an essential part of the Russian tea tradition for three centuries.

prompt: Complete: Пушкин — великий русский ___.
hints: He wrote 'Eugene Onegin'. Russia's most celebrated national poet.
culturalNote: Alexander Pushkin (1799–1837) is considered the founder of the modern Russian literary language.

prompt: a religious icon (painting)
hints: Sacred images central to the Russian Orthodox tradition — not a phone app icon!

prompt: Say: I want to visit the Hermitage.
hints: 'Хочу' + infinitive = I want to do something. 'Посетить' is the perfective infinitive.
culturalNote: The Hermitage in St Petersburg is one of the largest museums in the world, holding more than 3 million items.

prompt: the word for history (and also story)
hints: Careful — the same word covers both 'history' and 'story' in Russian!

prompt: What is a матрёшка?
hints: Open it up and find a smaller one inside — and again, and again.

prompt: the word for 'culture'
hints: A Latin-origin word used in many European languages — can you spot the pattern?

prompt: Say: In Russia, people often drink tea with jam.
correctAnswer: В России часто пьют чай с вареньем.
hints: 'Пьют' = they drink (3rd-person plural, used impersonally). 'С вареньем' = with jam (instrumental case).
culturalNote: Tea is central to Russian hospitality and is often served with jam, honey, or sweets.

prompt: Say: This building is very old.
hints: Describe the place.

prompt: Say: The guide speaks very clearly.
hints: Talk about a guide.

prompt: Say: Maslenitsa is a spring festival in Russia.
correctAnswer: Масленица — это весенний праздник в России.
hints: Use a dash instead of 'is' when both subject and predicate are nouns in Russian.
culturalNote: Maslenitsa is celebrated in the week before Lent; the main treat is bliny (pancakes).

prompt: Say: I visited the Tretyakov Gallery last summer.
correctAnswer: Прошлым летом я посетил Третьяковскую галерею.
hints: 'Посетил' is perfective past — a completed single visit.
culturalNote: The Tretyakov Gallery in Moscow holds the world's largest collection of Russian art.

prompt: Fill in the blank: В России принято снимать ___ при входе в дом.
hints: 'Обувь' = footwear/shoes. Taking shoes off at the door is a widespread Russian custom.

prompt: Ask: When was this church built?
hints: Passive past: 'была построена' = was built. 'Церковь' is feminine.

prompt: Say: Peter the Great founded Saint Petersburg.
hints: 'Основал' is the perfective past of 'основывать' = to found/establish.
culturalNote: St Petersburg was founded in 1703 and served as the capital of Russia until 1918.

prompt: Say: Russians celebrate New Year more than Christmas.
correctAnswer: Россияне отмечают Новый год больше, чем Рождество.
hints: 'Больше, чем' = more than. New Year is the main winter holiday in Russia.
culturalNote: Russia's New Year tree tradition carried over from the Soviet era, when religious Christmas celebrations were discouraged.

prompt: a folk tale
hints: Stories passed down through generations — think of Baba Yaga!
culturalNote: Baba Yaga is the most famous character in Russian folk tales.

prompt: Say: Tchaikovsky composed Swan Lake.
hints: 'Написал' (perfective past) = composed. Russian uses 'написал' for both written and musical works.
culturalNote: 'Swan Lake' (1876) is one of the most frequently performed ballets in the world.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Юрий Гагарин стал первым человеком в ___.
hints: 'Космос' = (outer) space. After 'в', use the prepositional case: 'космосе'.
culturalNote: On 12 April 1961, Yuri Gagarin made the first orbital flight around the Earth in history.

prompt: the revolution (as a historical event)
hints: 1917 saw two of them in Russia — February and October.

prompt: Ask: What is this statue made of?
hints: 'Из чего' = out of what. 'Сделана' is a short-form passive participle, feminine to agree with 'статуя'.

prompt: What is the Hermitage in Saint Petersburg?
hints: It began as the Winter Palace of the tsars — and grew from there.
culturalNote: The Hermitage occupies five historic buildings, including the Winter Palace.

prompt: Say: The White Nights happen in Saint Petersburg every summer.
correctAnswer: Белые ночи бывают в Санкт-Петербурге каждое лето.
hints: 'Бывают' = occur/take place regularly. 'Каждое лето' = every summer (accusative time expression).
culturalNote: The White Nights are a period from May to July when the sun barely sets in St Petersburg.

prompt: a cathedral
hints: Larger and grander than a church. Saint Basil's on Red Square is a famous example.
culturalNote: St Basil's Cathedral on Red Square is a 16th-century architectural masterpiece.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Большой театр известен своими постановками балета и ___.
hints: What else besides ballet does the Bolshoi stage? Think singing.
culturalNote: The Bolshoi Theater was founded in 1776 and is one of the world's leading opera and ballet houses.

prompt: Say: Victory Day is celebrated on 9 May.
hints: 'Отмечается' = is celebrated (impersonal passive). '9 мая' = the ninth of May.
culturalNote: Victory Day is one of Russia's main public holidays, marking the victory in the Second World War.

prompt: In which year did the Russian Revolution occur?
hints: There were actually two revolutions that year — February and October.
culturalNote: The October Revolution of 1917 led to the establishment of Soviet rule and radically changed the course of world history.

prompt: Choose the best response. Why is this old town important?
correctAnswer: Он показывает, как люди жили здесь много веков назад.
answerEnglish: It shows how people lived here many centuries ago.
hints: Answer like a guide.

prompt: Choose the best response. Who built this castle?
hints: Answer simply and clearly.

prompt: a tradition
hints: Match the cultural term.

prompt: Say: Russian literature has had a profound influence on world culture.
correctAnswer: Русская литература оказала огромное влияние на мировую культуру.
hints: 'Оказать влияние на' = to have an influence on. Accusative after 'на'.
culturalNote: Tolstoy, Dostoevsky, and Chekhov are among the most widely translated writers in the world.

prompt: Say: The Trans-Siberian Railway connects Moscow to Vladivostok.
correctAnswer: Транссибирская магистраль соединяет Москву с Владивостоком.
hints: 'Соединять с' = to connect with. 'С Владивостоком' is in the instrumental case.
culturalNote: The Trans-Siberian Railway is the longest railway in the world (about 9,288 km).

prompt: Say: Banya is an important part of Russian culture.
hints: Russian dash replaces 'is' between two nouns. 'Русской' = genitive feminine adjective.
culturalNote: The banya is a traditional Russian bathhouse, often visited together with family or friends.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Пасха является важнейшим православным ___.
hints: 'Праздник' = holiday/festival. After 'является' (is/constitutes), use the instrumental case: 'праздником'.

prompt: Say: The Soviet era left a lasting mark on Russian architecture.
correctAnswer: Советская эпоха оставила глубокий след в российской архитектуре.
hints: 'Оставить след в' = to leave a mark on. 'В архитектуре' uses the prepositional case.

prompt: Ask: What does this symbol represent in Russian culture?
correctAnswer: Что символизирует этот знак в русской культуре?
hints: 'Символизировать' = to symbolise. 'В русской культуре' = prepositional case.

prompt: Say: Lake Baikal is the deepest lake in the world.
correctAnswer: Байкал — самое глубокое озеро в мире.
hints: Superlatives in Russian: 'самое глубокое' = the deepest (neuter adjective agreeing with 'озеро').
culturalNote: Lake Baikal holds about 20% of the planet's fresh water and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

prompt: Say: Pushkin is considered the founder of modern Russian literature.
correctAnswer: Пушкин считается основоположником современной русской литературы.
hints: 'Считается' + instrumental = is considered to be. 'Основоположником' is instrumental of 'основоположник'.
culturalNote: Pushkin's novel in verse 'Eugene Onegin' is often called an encyclopedia of 19th-century Russian life.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Эрмитаж хранит более трёх миллионов ___ искусства.
hints: 'Произведение искусства' = work of art. 'Миллионов произведений' — genitive plural after a numeral.

prompt: Ask: Is photography allowed inside?
hints: 'Разрешено' + infinitive = is it permitted to. A practical question in any Russian museum.

prompt: Fill in the blank: В 1961 году Гагарин первым ___ в открытый космос.
hints: Perfective past of 'лететь' — 'полетел' captures a single, completed journey.
culturalNote: Gagarin's words 'Poyekhali!' ('Let's go!') became a symbol of the space age.

prompt: Which Russian festival is most associated with bliny (pancakes)?
hints: This spring festival precedes the Orthodox Lent — and involves a lot of butter.
culturalNote: Bliny symbolize the sun at Maslenitsa — golden, round, and hot.

prompt: Say: The Golden Ring is a group of ancient cities north-east of Moscow.
correctAnswer: Золотое кольцо — это группа древних городов к северо-востоку от Москвы.
hints: 'Группа городов' = a group of cities (genitive plural). 'К северо-востоку от' = to the north-east of.
culturalNote: The Golden Ring towns — Suzdal, Vladimir, and Rostov — preserve masterpieces of medieval architecture.

prompt: Fill in the blank: В Москве есть семь сталинских ___, которые называют «сёстрами».
hints: 'Высотка' = skyscraper (colloquial). 'Семь высоток' — genitive plural after the numeral.
culturalNote: The seven Stalinist skyscrapers (the 'Seven Sisters') were built in 1947–1953 and still shape Moscow's skyline today.

prompt: Say: This fresco dates back to the 12th century.
hints: 'Датироваться' + instrumental = to date from. 'XII веком' is the instrumental: 'the 12th century'.

prompt: Say: The birch tree is a symbol of Russia.
hints: Noun + dash + noun: no verb 'is' needed. 'Берёза' is the subject, 'символ' the predicate noun.
culturalNote: The birch is celebrated in Russian poetry and folk songs as an image of the homeland.

prompt: Say: Dostoyevsky explored the psychology of guilt in Crime and Punishment.
correctAnswer: Достоевский исследовал психологию вины в «Преступлении и наказании».
hints: 'Исследовать' = to explore/investigate. 'Психологию вины' is the accusative object.
culturalNote: 'Crime and Punishment' (1866) has been translated into more than 170 languages.

prompt: Say: Could you explain this tradition to me?
hints: A polite question about local culture.

prompt: Say: I love learning local history.
hints: A positive statement.

prompt: Say: This legend is popular here.
hints: Use a natural tone.

prompt: Say: Russian Orthodox Christianity shaped the country's cultural identity for centuries.
correctAnswer: Русское православное христианство формировало культурную идентичность страны на протяжении веков.
hints: 'На протяжении' + genitive = over the course of. Imperfective 'формировало' stresses the ongoing process.
culturalNote: The Russian Orthodox Church has had a profound influence on art, architecture, and social customs.

prompt: Say: The intelligentsia played a crucial role in shaping pre-revolutionary Russia.
correctAnswer: Интеллигенция сыграла ключевую роль в формировании дореволюционной России.
hints: 'Сыграть роль в' = to play a role in. 'В формировании' — verbal noun in prepositional case.

prompt: Say: Soviet propaganda transformed the way Russians perceived their own history.
correctAnswer: Советская пропаганда изменила то, как россияне воспринимали свою собственную историю.
hints: 'То, как' introduces a clause meaning 'the way in which'. Perfective 'изменила' = transformed (fully).

prompt: Fill in the blank: Россия обладает богатым культурным ___, включающим литературу, музыку и балет.
hints: 'Наследие' = heritage. After 'обладать' (to possess), use the instrumental case: 'наследием'.

prompt: Say: Constructivism was a revolutionary art movement that emerged in post-1917 Russia.
correctAnswer: Конструктивизм — революционное художественное движение, возникшее в России после 1917 года.
hints: 'Возникшее' is an active past participle (short relative clause) agreeing with 'движение' (neuter).
culturalNote: Constructivism influenced graphic design, architecture, and industrial production around the world.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Изучение архивных ___ позволяет реконструировать жизнь прошлых эпох.
hints: 'Исторический источник' = historical source. 'Источников' is the genitive plural after 'архивных'.

prompt: Say: The Silver Age of Russian poetry flourished at the turn of the 19th and 20th centuries.
correctAnswer: Серебряный век русской поэзии расцвёл на рубеже XIX–XX веков.
hints: 'На рубеже' = at the turn of. 'Расцвёл' = bloomed/flourished (perfective past of 'расцветать').
culturalNote: The Silver Age gave the world poets such as Akhmatova, Blok, Pasternak, and Tsvetaeva.

prompt: Say: Imperial Russia's eastward expansion shaped both its culture and its conflicts.
correctAnswer: Экспансия Российской империи на восток определила как её культуру, так и её конфликты.
hints: 'Как…, так и…' = both… and…. 'Определила' = shaped/determined (perfective past, feminine).

prompt: Fill in the blank: Достоевский исследует нравственные ___ своих персонажей в глубоко психологических текстах.
hints: 'Нравственная дилемма' = moral dilemma. Accusative plural after 'исследует'.

prompt: Ask: How did this region's history differ from the rest of the country?
correctAnswer: Чем история этого региона отличалась от истории остальной страны?
hints: 'Чем … отличаться от' = to differ in what way from. 'Остальной страны' = genitive of 'остальная страна'.

prompt: Say: The Fabergé eggs represent the pinnacle of Russian imperial craftsmanship.
correctAnswer: Яйца Фаберже — вершина мастерства русских императорских ювелиров.
hints: 'Вершина чего-то' = the pinnacle of something. 'Ювелиров' = genitive plural of 'ювелир' (jeweller).
culturalNote: Carl Fabergé created 50 imperial eggs for Alexander III and Nicholas II between 1885 and 1916.

prompt: Fill in the blank: Советское наследие продолжает ___ на российскую политику и общественную жизнь.
hints: 'Продолжать' + imperfective infinitive = to continue doing something. 'Влиять на' = to influence.

prompt: Say: Eisenstein's films pioneered the use of montage as a political and artistic tool.
correctAnswer: Фильмы Эйзенштейна были новаторскими в использовании монтажа как политического и художественного инструмента.
hints: 'Новаторский' = pioneering/innovative. 'В использовании' = in the use of (prepositional verbal noun).
culturalNote: 'Battleship Potemkin' (1925) is regarded as one of the greatest films of all time.

prompt: Say: The concept of 'the Russian soul' is celebrated by some and contested by others.
correctAnswer: Концепция «русской души» одними прославляется, другими оспаривается.
hints: 'Одними … другими' = by some … by others (instrumental). Two short-form passive participles in parallel.
